\begin{document}

\title{Sparse 3\textdegree{} Node Enrichment in Eurasian Monument Corpora}

\author{Seth Tenenbaum\thanks{ORCID: \href{https://orcid.org/0009-0008-5797-2498}{0009-0008-5797-2498}.
Correspondence: seth@fourthtemple.com}\\
Independent Scholar, USA}

\maketitle

\abstract{
We report a two-stage observational study of Eurasian monument
geography. In the exploratory stage, domed and stupa-related sites
within the UNESCO Cultural and Mixed World Heritage corpus
($N=\NclusterTotal$) showed elevated node-neighborhood clustering
conditional on a specified 3\textdegree{} / $\sim$35\textdegree{}E
longitude frame: modest full-corpus Tier-A+ enrichment with stronger
dome/stupa behavior, and the dome/stupa signal locates in the subset's
own longitude distribution (Null~A, $p=\geoNullDomeBootP$). In the
validation stage, the same frame was applied without modification to
the externally curated Wikidata Q180987 stupa corpus ($N=\vmNStupa{}$),
assembled independently of the UNESCO analysis. A Normalized Phasor
Consensus (NPC) test finds that UNESCO Cultural/Mixed, the nested
UNESCO dome subset, and the Wikidata stupa corpus recover concordant
3\textdegree{} anchors ($K=\npcMonK{}$, perm-$p=\npcMonPermP{}$),
with a consensus grid line within \npcMonGridDeltaE\textdegree{} of
35\textdegree{}E; OWTRAD Silk Road route nodes do not share this
anchor. The finding is sparse node-neighborhood enrichment, not
whole-corpus alignment: most sites do not fall near harmonic nodes and
the full-corpus Rayleigh periodogram is null ($p=\fullRayleighPermP$).
Because the UNESCO keyword filter was constructed with knowledge of the
data, its conditional enrichment results are treated as exploratory;
the Wikidata cross-corpus NPC concordance is the primary
external-validation evidence. The result is bounded by the null global
periodogram, marginal anchor-discovery correction, geographic
dependence, and the absence of evidence for universal periodicity,
historical intentionality, metrology, or a unique Gerizim origin.
}

\bigskip

\noindent\textbf{Keywords:}
template alignment, spatial statistics, Levant, UNESCO World Heritage,
3-degree harmonics, selected-frame analysis, circular statistics,
monument distribution, anchor consensus

\bigskip
